metadata.title: Tadasana
metadata.description: A steady standing yoga practice that improves posture, alignment, and supported balance during pregnancy.

benefits: Improves upright alignment while practicing prenatal mountain pose.
Strengthens postural control without using forceful movement.
Helps distribute body weight more evenly through the feet.
Encourages confidence in stable, supported standing positions.
instructions: Stand near a wall or chair so one hand can reach support when needed.
Enter prenatal mountain pose with the feet placed steadily and the spine tall.
Keep the chest open and the knees soft instead of locking the joints.
Move out of the posture slowly to maintain balance and control.
safetyTips: Practice near a wall or chair if your balance feels less steady today.
Avoid overreaching the stance or forcing depth in the pose.
Keep transitions slow because sudden shifts can feel unstable in pregnancy.
Stop immediately if you feel dizziness, pain, or unusual shortness of breath.

metadata.title: DeviAsana
metadata.description: A gentle prenatal yoga activity that supports safe movement, comfort, and body awareness.

benefits: Supports safe prenatal movement through supported goddess pose.
Improves body awareness in a controlled, manageable way.
Encourages comfort and ease during light physical activity.
Helps maintain a gentle activity routine through pregnancy.
instructions: Begin in a stable, supported position that feels easy to maintain.
Practice supported goddess pose with slow and mindful movement.
Keep your breath steady and avoid pushing into discomfort.
Pause whenever needed and finish with a gradual return to rest.
safetyTips: Move only within a comfortable and pain-free range.
Use support whenever the posture feels unstable or tiring.
Avoid overheating and keep water nearby during practice.
Stop immediately if you feel dizziness, pain, or unusual shortness of breath.

metadata.title: WarriorPose
metadata.description: A supportive standing yoga pose that gently opens the body, improves stability, and promotes smooth mobility.

benefits: Gently opens the lower body through chair-supported warrior i.
Supports leg and hip engagement with controlled posture.
Improves steadiness while changing weight from one side to another.
Encourages better body alignment during standing activity.
instructions: Place your feet securely and use a chair, wall, or blocks if the pose includes support.
Set up chair-supported warrior i with a long spine and a comfortable stance width.
Keep the movement open through the chest and avoid collapsing into the lower back.
Return to neutral posture slowly after each side or hold.
safetyTips: Practice near a wall or chair if your balance feels less steady today.
Avoid overreaching the stance or forcing depth in the pose.
Keep transitions slow because sudden shifts can feel unstable in pregnancy.
Stop immediately if you feel dizziness, pain, or unusual shortness of breath.

metadata.title: CatCowFlow
metadata.description: A flowing prenatal yoga movement that supports spinal, pelvic, and hip mobility with low strain.

benefits: Improves gentle movement quality during cat-cow flow.
Helps release stiffness in the spine, hips, or pelvis.
Supports easier transitions between positions in daily life.
Encourages fluid motion rather than rigid, guarded movement.
instructions: Start from a stable base so the movement can stay slow and controlled.
Flow through cat-cow flow in time with your breathing pattern.
Keep the range of motion comfortable instead of chasing a bigger movement.
Repeat evenly on both sides or through both directions where applicable.
safetyTips: Use props or support whenever the movement feels too deep or unstable.
Avoid compressing the belly or pushing into sharp stretching sensations.
Keep the pace slow so alignment remains controlled throughout the practice.
Stop immediately if you feel dizziness, pain, or unusual shortness of breath.

metadata.title: MulaBreath
metadata.description: A gentle breathing-based yoga practice that supports calm focus, steadier breath, and body awareness.

benefits: Encourages slower, more controlled breathing during seated pelvic floor breathing.
Helps settle mental restlessness and promotes a calmer state.
Supports better oxygen flow with a soft, sustainable rhythm.
Builds awareness of how breath and posture work together.
instructions: Sit or rest in a supported position where your shoulders can stay relaxed.
Begin seated pelvic floor breathing with a soft inhale through the nose and an unforced exhale.
Keep the jaw, neck, and belly relaxed as the breathing rhythm settles.
Continue at an easy pace and stop before the breath starts to feel strained.
safetyTips: Do not hold the breath forcefully or create pressure while breathing.
Keep the practice gentle, especially if nasal breathing feels restricted.
Avoid any breathing pattern that causes light-headedness or chest discomfort.
Stop immediately if you feel dizziness, pain, or unusual shortness of breath.

metadata.title: VishramSide
metadata.description: A restorative prenatal yoga sequence that eases tension, encourages relaxation, and helps the body settle comfortably.

benefits: Allows the body to soften and recover during supported savasana (side-lying).
Reduces built-up muscular tension through supported positioning.
Encourages relaxation in the back, hips, and rib area.
Creates a quiet recovery window that can ease physical fatigue.
instructions: Set up pillows, folded blankets, or a chair so the body feels fully supported.
Ease into supported savasana (side-lying) slowly and adjust the position until there is no pinching or pressure.
Let the breath become quiet and natural rather than trying to deepen it forcefully.
Stay only as long as the posture feels restful, then come out gradually.
safetyTips: Use enough support under the head, knees, or side body to stay comfortable.
Avoid lying flat on the back for long periods if it feels uncomfortable.
Change position slowly so blood pressure does not drop suddenly.
Stop immediately if you feel dizziness, pain, or unusual shortness of breath.

metadata.title: NeckFlow
metadata.description: A focused prenatal stretch that relieves tightness, improves range of motion, and encourages easier daily movement.

benefits: Targets local tightness effectively during seated neck release.
Improves flexibility with a controlled and manageable stretch.
Helps reduce pulling sensations caused by inactivity or posture.
Encourages smoother everyday movement with less stiffness.
instructions: Set your position first so the target area can lengthen without strain.
Move into seated neck release gradually until a mild stretch is felt.
Keep breathing smoothly and avoid bouncing or pulsing the stretch.
Release slowly and allow the area to soften before repeating.
safetyTips: Use props or support whenever the movement feels too deep or unstable.
Avoid compressing the belly or pushing into sharp stretching sensations.
Keep the pace slow so alignment remains controlled throughout the practice.
Stop immediately if you feel dizziness, pain, or unusual shortness of breath.
